title: Cargo: 36 transitive dep duplications (multiple_crate_versions)
description:
Question: 

Context: 

Findings: 

**Dimension:** Performance & Complexity
**Lint:** clippy::multiple_crate_versions

## Violation
36 crates have multiple versions in the dep tree (bitflags 1.3.2 + 2.11.1, block-buffer 0.10 + 0.12, core-foundation 0.9 + 0.10, cpufeatures 0.2 + 0.3, crypto-common 0.1 + 0.2, darling 0.20 + 0.23, etc.). Each duplication inflates build time and binary size; some cause subtle API-incompatibility bugs when types from different versions are mixed.

## Mandated Refactor
1. Run `cargo tree -d` to see each duplicate's dependency path.
2. For each duplicate, identify which direct dep pulls in the old version. Common culprits: outdated versions of crypto crates held back by `sha2` or `hmac`, and `darling` held back by `serde_derive`-family crates.
3. File follow-up upstream-pins when no direct fix exists (e.g., waiting for `foo` to bump `bar`).
4. Candidates to audit: bitflags (likely `rusqlite` vs others), hashbrown, darling, windows-sys family. Some are unavoidable today; note which.

## Scope note
This is research rather than a single refactor because resolution depends on upstream crates. Expect to split this into per-duplicate follow-up issues.
